title_en: The City of the King of Justice
context: Lord Vishnu describes Yamapuri, the magnificent city of Dharmaraja (Yama), the role of Chitragupta as the divine recorder of all deeds, and the process by which souls are judged according to their karma. The weighing of merits and sins determines the soul's fate.

verses.hindi:
श्रीगरुड उवाच —
यमस्य नगरं देव कीदृशं तद्वदस्व मे ।
चित्रगुप्तश्च धर्मात्मा कथं कर्माणि वेत्ति हि ॥
verses.transliteration:
Śrī Garuḍa uvāca —
Yamasya nagaraṃ deva kīdṛśaṃ tad vadasva me |
Citraguptaśca dharmātmā kathaṃ karmāṇi vetti hi ||
verses.translation: Garuda said: O Lord, describe to me the city of Yama. And how does the righteous Chitragupta know the deeds of all beings?

verses.hindi:
श्रीभगवानुवाच —
शृणु तार्क्ष्य प्रवक्ष्यामि यमस्य नगरं महत् ।
चतुर्द्वारं विशालं च लोहप्राकारसंयुतम् ॥
verses.transliteration:
Śrī Bhagavān uvāca —
Śṛṇu Tārkṣya pravakṣyāmi yamasya nagaraṃ mahat |
caturdvāraṃ viśālaṃ ca lohaprākārasaṃyutam ||
verses.translation: The Lord said: Listen, O Tarksya, I shall describe the great city of Yama. It has four gates, is vast and wide, and is surrounded by walls of iron.

verses.hindi:
विस्तीर्णा दशयोजनं समन्तात् परिमण्डला ।
वज्रमय्या सभा तत्र यमस्य परमाद्भुता ॥
verses.transliteration:
Vistīrṇā daśayojanaṃ samantāt parimaṇḍalā |
vajramayyā sabhā tatra yamasya paramādbhutā ||
verses.translation: It extends ten yojanas in every direction, perfectly circular. Within it stands the court of Yama, made of adamant, supremely wondrous.

verses.hindi:
तत्र सिंहासने दिव्ये धर्मराजो यमः स्थितः ।
महिषारूढो विरूपो दण्डहस्तो भयंकरः ॥
verses.transliteration:
Tatra siṃhāsane divye Dharmarājo Yamaḥ sthitaḥ |
mahiṣārūḍho virūpo daṇḍahasto bhayaṃkaraḥ ||
verses.translation: There, upon a divine throne, sits Dharmaraja Yama, riding a buffalo, of fearsome form, holding a staff of punishment, terrifying to behold.

verses.hindi:
कृष्णवर्णो महाकायो रक्तनेत्रो भयप्रदः ।
कालपाशधरो रौद्रो ज्वालामालासमावृतः ॥
verses.transliteration:
Kṛṣṇavarṇo mahākāyo raktanetro bhayapradaḥ |
kālapāśadharo raudro jvālāmālāsamāvṛtaḥ ||
verses.translation: He is dark-complexioned, of immense body, with red eyes, fear-inspiring, bearing the noose of time, fierce, and surrounded by garlands of flame.

verses.hindi:
तस्य पार्श्वे स्थितो नित्यं चित्रगुप्तः प्रतापवान् ।
सर्वजीवकृतं कर्म लेखयन् पत्रिकासु च ॥
verses.transliteration:
Tasya pārśve sthito nityaṃ Citraguptaḥ pratāpavān |
sarvajīvakṛtaṃ karma lekhayan patrikāsu ca ||
verses.translation: At his side stands eternally the powerful Chitragupta, recording on scrolls every deed done by every living being.

verses.hindi:
चित्रगुप्तो महाबुद्धिर्ब्रह्मणो देहसम्भवः ।
लेखनीं धारयन् हस्ते पुस्तकं चापि दक्षिणे ॥
verses.transliteration:
Citragupto mahābuddhir Brahmaṇo dehasambhavaḥ |
lekhanīṃ dhārayan haste pustakaṃ cāpi dakṣiṇe ||
verses.translation: Chitragupta, of great intelligence, born from the body of Brahma, holds a pen in one hand and a book in the other.

verses.hindi:
सर्वेषां प्राणिनां कर्म पुण्यं पापं च सर्वशः ।
सूर्याचन्द्रमसौ साक्षी वायुरग्निश्च पञ्चमः ॥
verses.transliteration:
Sarveṣāṃ prāṇināṃ karma puṇyaṃ pāpaṃ ca sarvaśaḥ |
Sūryā-candramasau sākṣī Vāyur Agniśca pañcamaḥ ||
verses.translation: Of all living beings, every merit and every sin is witnessed. The Sun and Moon are witnesses, and Wind and Fire are the fifth.

verses.hindi:
दिवारात्रिस्तथाकाशं सन्ध्ये धर्मस्तथैव च ।
आपो भूमिस्तथात्मा च साक्षिणो धर्मकर्मणाम् ॥
verses.transliteration:
Divā-rātris tathā-ākāśaṃ sandhye dharmas tathaiva ca |
āpo bhūmis tathātmā ca sākṣiṇo dharmakarmāṇām ||
verses.translation: Day and Night, Space, the two Twilights, Dharma itself, Water, Earth, and the Soul — all are witnesses to righteous and sinful deeds.

verses.hindi:
तैः साक्षिभिः समानीतं कर्म शुभाशुभं नृणाम् ।
चित्रगुप्तो विचार्यैव यमं विज्ञापयेत् ततः ॥
verses.transliteration:
Taiḥ sākṣibhiḥ samānītaṃ karma śubhāśubhaṃ nṛṇām |
Citragupto vicāryaiva Yamaṃ vijñāpayet tataḥ ||
verses.translation: Through these witnesses, the good and evil deeds of humans are brought forth. Chitragupta, having examined them, then reports to Yama.

verses.hindi:
ततो धर्मासने दिव्ये यमो धर्मेण पश्यति ।
तुलां निधाय कर्माणि पुण्यं पापं च वीक्षते ॥
verses.transliteration:
Tato dharmāsane divye Yamo dharmeṇa paśyati |
tulāṃ nidhāya karmāṇi puṇyaṃ pāpaṃ ca vīkṣate ||
verses.translation: Then from his divine seat of justice, Yama judges according to dharma. Placing the deeds on a balance, he examines both merit and sin.

verses.hindi:
यस्य पुण्यं बहुतरं पापं चाल्पतरं भवेत् ।
स स्वर्गं लभते जन्तुर्देवलोकं स गच्छति ॥
verses.transliteration:
Yasya puṇyaṃ bahutaraṃ pāpaṃ cālpataraṃ bhavet |
sa svargaṃ labhate jantur devalokaṃ sa gacchati ||
verses.translation: The being whose merit is greater and whose sin is lesser attains heaven and goes to the realm of the gods.

verses.hindi:
यस्य पापं बहुतरं पुण्यं चाल्पतरं भवेत् ।
स नरकं लभते जन्तुर्यातनां प्राप्य दुःसहाम् ॥
verses.transliteration:
Yasya pāpaṃ bahutaraṃ puṇyaṃ cālpataraṃ bhavet |
sa narakaṃ labhate jantur yātanāṃ prāpya duḥsahām ||
verses.translation: The being whose sin is greater and whose merit is lesser attains hell and endures unbearable torments.

verses.hindi:
समं यस्य भवेत् कर्म पुण्यपापं तु मानवे ।
स मानुष्यं लभेज्जन्तुर्मर्त्यलोकं प्रपद्यते ॥
verses.transliteration:
Samaṃ yasya bhavet karma puṇyapāpaṃ tu mānave |
sa mānuṣyaṃ labhej jantur martyalokaṃ prapadyate ||
verses.translation: The being whose merit and sin are equal attains human birth again and returns to the mortal world.

verses.hindi:
चित्रगुप्तो महाराज तिलमात्रं न मुञ्चति ।
सर्वं लिखति यत् कर्म जन्मप्रभृति मानवः ॥
verses.transliteration:
Citragupto mahārāja tilamātraṃ na muñcati |
sarvaṃ likhati yat karma janmaprabhṛti mānavaḥ ||
verses.translation: Chitragupta, O great king, does not leave out even a sesame seed's worth. He records every deed from the moment of birth.

verses.hindi:
मनसा चिन्तितं कर्म वचसा निश्चितं तथा ।
शरीरेण कृतं कर्म तत्सर्वं लिख्यते ध्रुवम् ॥
verses.transliteration:
Manasā cintitaṃ karma vacasā niścitaṃ tathā |
śarīreṇa kṛtaṃ karma tat sarvaṃ likhyate dhruvam ||
verses.translation: Deeds contemplated by the mind, resolved by speech, and performed by the body — all of these are assuredly recorded.

verses.hindi:
आनीतं यमदूतैस्तु जीवं भीतं कृपानकम् ।
सभायां धर्मराजस्य स्थापयन्ति पुरो बलात् ॥
verses.transliteration:
Ānītaṃ yamadūtaistu jīvaṃ bhītaṃ kṛpānakam |
sabhāyāṃ Dharmarājasya sthāpayanti puro balāt ||
verses.translation: The soul, frightened and pitiable, brought by the messengers of Yama, is forcibly placed before the court of the King of Justice.

verses.hindi:
ततो धर्मो वदेत् तं तु किं कृतं सुकृतं त्वया ।
किं कृतं दुष्कृतं चैव वद सत्यं ममाग्रतः ॥
verses.transliteration:
Tato Dharmo vadet taṃ tu kiṃ kṛtaṃ sukṛtaṃ tvayā |
kiṃ kṛtaṃ duṣkṛtaṃ caiva vada satyaṃ mamāgrataḥ ||
verses.translation: Then Dharma (Yama) speaks to the soul: 'What good deeds have you done? And what evil? Speak the truth before me.'

verses.hindi:
मिथ्यावाक्यं वदेद्यस्तु यमस्याग्रे नराधमः ।
तप्तलोहमयं सूत्रं जिह्वायां चास्य बध्यते ॥
verses.transliteration:
Mithyāvākyaṃ vaded yastu yamasyāgre narādhamaḥ |
taptalohamayaṃ sūtraṃ jihvāyāṃ cāsya badhyate ||
verses.translation: If the lowest of men speaks false words before Yama, a thread of heated iron is bound upon his tongue.

verses.hindi:
सर्वं विदितमेवास्ति चित्रगुप्तस्य लेखनात् ।
न शक्यं गोपनं कर्तुं कर्म किञ्चन मानवैः ॥
verses.transliteration:
Sarvaṃ viditam evāsti Citraguptasya lekhanāt |
na śakyaṃ gopanaṃ kartuṃ karma kiñcana mānavaiḥ ||
verses.translation: Everything is already known from Chitragupta's records. No deed whatsoever can be concealed by mortals.

verses.hindi:
यथा गवां सहस्रेषु वत्सो विन्दति मातरम् ।
तथा पूर्वकृतं कर्म कर्तारमनुगच्छति ॥
verses.transliteration:
Yathā gavāṃ sahasreṣu vatso vindati mātaram |
tathā pūrvakṛtaṃ karma kartāram anugacchati ||
verses.translation: As among thousands of cows a calf finds its own mother, so does past karma follow its doer.

verses.hindi:
ततो यमो विचार्यैव कर्म शुभाशुभं नृणाम् ।
दण्डं ददाति पापेभ्यो धर्मिणां सुगतिं तथा ॥
verses.transliteration:
Tato Yamo vicāryaiva karma śubhāśubhaṃ nṛṇām |
daṇḍaṃ dadāti pāpebhyo dharmiṇāṃ sugatiṃ tathā ||
verses.translation: Then Yama, having examined the good and evil deeds of mortals, gives punishment to sinners and a good destination to the righteous.

verses.hindi:
यमो हि धर्मराजस्तु न पक्षपातमाचरेत् ।
कर्मणैव फलं दद्यान्न मित्रं न च बान्धवम् ॥
verses.transliteration:
Yamo hi Dharmarājastu na pakṣapātam ācaret |
karmaṇaiva phalaṃ dadyān na mitraṃ na ca bāndhavam ||
verses.translation: Yama the King of Justice never shows partiality. He gives the fruit according to karma alone — there is no friend, no kinsman there.

verses.hindi:
राजा वा यदि वा दासो धनी वा निर्धनोऽपि वा ।
समं विचार्यते कर्म धर्मराजसभातले ॥
verses.transliteration:
Rājā vā yadi vā dāso dhanī vā nirdhano'pi vā |
samaṃ vicāryate karma Dharmarājasabhātale ||
verses.translation: Whether king or servant, wealthy or destitute — all karma is judged equally on the floor of Dharmaraja's court.

verses.hindi:
श्रीभगवानुवाच —
तस्मात् कर्म शुभं कुर्यात् धर्मं सत्यं च पालयेत् ।
यमस्य सभया तात भीतो भव सदा नरः ॥
verses.transliteration:
Śrī Bhagavān uvāca —
Tasmāt karma śubhaṃ kuryāt dharmaṃ satyaṃ ca pālayet |
Yamasya sabhayā tāta bhīto bhava sadā naraḥ ||
verses.translation: The Lord said: Therefore one should perform good deeds and uphold dharma and truth. O dear one, a person should always be mindful of Yama's court.
